markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create tests proving CRDT updates promote to EventLedger exactly once and remain replayable. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: CrdtEventLedgerPromotionTests leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CrdtEventLedgerPromotionTests is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 3c645328; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: EXACTLY-ONCE IS REAL, NOT SCHEDULING LUCK. The e2e test (knowledge_crdt_proof_tests.rs:90-370) promotes once, then promotes AGAIN with the same proposal id and asserts AlreadyPromoted with the SAME fact_id (line 277-282), then counts the ledger and asserts EXACTLY one PromotionRequested and one PromotionAccepted (line 287-300). The guarantee underneath is structural: get_promoted_fact_by_proposal short-circuits (claim_promotion.rs:91), ON CONFLICT(proposal_id) DO NOTHING on the fact (knowledge_crdt.rs:833), and fixed idempotency keys dedup the event pair. This holds under same-proposal hammering. PASS on the exactly-once claim itself.

TEST UNWITTINGLY DEMONSTRATES THE MT-069 AUTHORITY HOLE (high). At line 227 the promoted claim cites source_span_refs = ['pending:<crdt_doc>:full-doc'] -- a SOFT REF to a span that does NOT exist in knowledge_spans -- and the test asserts the promotion SUCCEEDS (Promoted) and lands a knowledge_crdt_promoted_facts authority row. So this 'proof' is direct evidence of the dangling-evidence authority seam (see MT-069 review): a non-existent span becomes durable authority and the suite treats it as the happy path. The exactly-once proof is sound but it is built on top of a violation of 'claims MUST carry evidence spans'.

REPLAY CONVERGENCE CLAIM IS SCOPED TOO BROADLY. The test proves byte-identical RECONSTRUCTION of the DRAFT log (build_crdt_replay_plan + verify_causal_chain + per-update sha256 equality, lines 326-342) and snapshot-bounded replay (line 350-356) -- all of which are genuine and pass. But it does NOT prove that passive EventLedger replay reconstructs the promoted FACT after a crash between the event pair and the fact insert (the MT-069 #2 crash window). 'idempotent replays converge on the same fact id' is demonstrated only via active re-promotion (calling promote_graph_proposal again), never via pure ledger replay. The crash-window non-convergence (MT-069 concern #2) is not covered.

Stale/duplicate idempotency legs are well covered: duplicate push -> AlreadyStored (line 138-144), stale base -> Denied (line 160-163), duplicate ledger append with same idempotency key -> same event_id (line 304-317), denied/stale pushes never land (records.len()==3, line 324).
evasion_probes: Concurrent same-proposal promotion: the test's sequential re-promote proves AlreadyPromoted; structurally the ON CONFLICT + fact short-circuit guarantee the same under true concurrency (only one fact row by PK/unique proposal_id). No double fact.
Probed whether the suite proves crash-window replay convergence: it does NOT -- convergence is only shown by re-invoking the promotion function, not by replaying the ledger into a fresh fact table.
Probed the span backing the promoted authority fact: it is a 'pending:' marker with no knowledge_spans row -> dangling evidence accepted as authority (corroborates MT-069 #1).
evidence_checked: Rerun status: test rerun deferred -- working tree carries another lane's uncommitted knowledge_code_index module wired into lib.rs:180, so cargo build of the test binaries is not safe; static review of the committed test source stands. POSTGRES_TEST_URL not set in review shell.

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (33 CRDT tests pass under runtime-full,test-utils).

evidence: Hardened d6adf2dd: Proof test FLIPPED from happy-path to refusal (was promoting a pending: span as authority); valid-span leg re-grounded on real fixture.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.
evidence_basis: Subagent and local WP Validator review of only the MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION at session read.
Direct packet status/dependency map checked before mutation.

Targeted local cargo probes passed for MT-054, MT-056, and MT-063 hardening paths: relationship_id_resists_separator_injection_collision; db_blocks_retired_to_accepted_resurrection_via_raw_sql; scratch_schema_apply_rollback_reapply.
MT-049..MT-064 were independently confirmed by the returned subagent slice using real PostgreSQL-backed test paths.
wp_validator_review_v1.residual_uncertainty: Validation was scoped to MTs already READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Concurrent WIP/untracked files outside those MTs were not inspected.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
